description: Paracetamol may increase the anticoagulant effect of warfarin with prolonged use.
Increased risk of liver damage when paracetamol is combined with alcohol.
Paracetamol (Ace) may increase anticoagulant effect of warfarin.
Esomeprazole significantly reduces the antiplatelet effect of clopidogrel.
Omeprazole significantly reduces the antiplatelet effect of clopidogrel.
Esomeprazole (Maxpro) significantly reduces the antiplatelet effect of clopidogrel.
PPIs may increase methotrexate levels by reducing renal clearance.
Cetirizine drowsiness is enhanced by alcohol consumption.
Chlorpheniramine causes significant drowsiness, worsened by alcohol.
Taking two antihistamines together may increase sedation.
Combining two antihistamines is generally unnecessary and may increase side effects.
Azithromycin may enhance the anticoagulant effect of warfarin.
Generally safe combination but monitor for increased liver burden.
Amoxicillin may reduce renal clearance of methotrexate, increasing toxicity risk.
Amoxicillin may enhance the anticoagulant effect of warfarin.
Cefixime may enhance the anticoagulant effect of warfarin.
Phenobarbital reduces the plasma concentration of montelukast.
Beta-blockers may antagonize the bronchodilator effect of salbutamol. Avoid combination in asthma.
PPIs may increase digoxin plasma levels by altering gastric pH.
Omeprazole may increase digoxin plasma levels by altering gastric pH.
Both are paracetamol brands. Taking together risks accidental overdose.
Napa Extra contains paracetamol + caffeine. Taking with Napa risks paracetamol overdose.
Both are PPIs. Taking two PPIs together provides no additional benefit.
Both are esomeprazole brands. Duplicate therapy provides no benefit.
Omeprazole (Losectil) significantly reduces the antiplatelet effect of clopidogrel.
Probenecid reduces renal clearance of acyclovir, increasing levels.
Theophylline may slightly reduce cetirizine clearance.
Ketoconazole increases fexofenadine plasma concentration.
Erythromycin increases fexofenadine plasma concentration.
Salbutamol may reduce serum digoxin levels.
Increased risk of hepatotoxicity when paracetamol is used with isoniazid.
